rulings.Case Title: SHARON DUNN et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Charles Dunn¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant (Cranite) was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue on this topic. Plaintiffs¿ request for judicial notice is denied. Plaintiffs¿ opposition exceeded the page limit in violation of CRC 3.1113. The Court only considered the first twenty pages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD PALERMO VS. ACE HARDWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint For Personal Injury (Asbestos)
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 11, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Autozone West, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ complaint related to punitive damages is denied. Plaintiffs¿ complaint sufficiently pleads facts showing the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
